creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: You Might Not Need 50 Diffusion Steps — Ziv Ilan, Nvidia
videos.summary: NVIDIA's approach reduces diffusion model denoising steps from fifty to as few as one by combining dynamic quantization, latent chunk caching to skip recomputation, and student-teacher distillation. The technique achieves ten to two hundred times speedup enabling near real-time video generation on Blackwell B200, with gains described as additive so methods can be applied independently or stacked.

videos.title: Why MCP and ChatGPT Apps Use Double Iframes — Frédéric Barthelet, Alpic
videos.summary: Double iframe pattern isolates third-party apps in ChatGPT and MCP by nesting an srcdoc iframe within a sandboxed iframe loaded from a controlled subdomain. This avoids CSP violations, prevents localStorage/cookie access, blocks sandbox escapes, and allows per-app subdomain isolation. All external domains must be declared in MCP metadata.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: Studio is an internal workspace by WorkOS that allows teams to ask questions against Snowflake, Linear, and Notion in natural language and get answers or reusable widgets. The LLM generates declarative JavaScript widgets that call underlying data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cheap.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a web standard proposed by Google that enables AI agents to interact with web applications through structured, named tools instead of navigating the DOM. It supports both declarative HTML attributes that auto-generate JSON schemas and imperative JavaScript APIs for complex multi-step flows.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Omnigent: The New Meta-Harness for EVERY Coding Agent - Claude Code, Codex, Pi, More
videos.summary: Omnigent is a meta-harness that orchestrates multiple coding agents like Claude Code, Codex, and Pi as interchangeable workers under a unified interface. It enables workflows where different models handle different tasks, with governance, human-in-the-loop controls, and cross-device session persistence.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the sudden removal of Claude Fable 5 by Anthropic following an apparent US government export-control directive, covering the claimed jailbreak that triggered the action and the implications for developers. He provides benchmarks comparing Fable 5 to Opus 4.8 before the model became unavailable.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open source tool that simplifies building and deploying production-grade AI agents. It provides identity management, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails for deployed agents, enabling rapid development and deployment workflows.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live Q&A session on Saturday covering AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The stream combines discussion with chess gameplay and is part of a weekly content cadence with videos dropping on Wednesdays.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: A video explains how to build proactive AI agents and self-improving company systems using autonomous loops. It covers use cases like autonomous ads optimization and SEO, with emphasis on memory and tools that enable these capabilities.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature in Crewlet and related tools for autonomous coding workflows. The video covers goal setup, prompting practices, and mission exploration for long-duration tasks, framing these as steps toward true autonomous coders.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is a new AI coding paradigm that integrates harness setup, auto-testing, and workflow management. The presentation covers how Symphony works, its setup process, and integration with development workflows through WORKFLOW.md configuration.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason reviews several autonomous agent systems and techniques including Autoresearch, AutoAgent, Claude Code's memory architecture, Hermes self-evolution, and Openclaw skill evolution. The video compares how these agents autonomously refine their behavior and knowledge.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series consisting of Nano, Super, and Ultra variants. The architecture combines hybrid Mamba transformer, Latent MoE, and MTP components to differentiate from other open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released the M3 language model featuring MiniMax Sparse Attention (MSA), a shift from full attention to sparse attention mechanisms. The model includes tiled I/O improvements and optimized KV cache reading strategies to reduce latency during prefill and decode inference stages.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: Video explanation of Pi, described as a unique agent harness that enables different use cases. The video compares Pi against frontier agents like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity, and discusses the Pi framework and its applications.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb discusses Huawei's announcement of the Tau Scaling Law as part of the semiconductor race between China and the USA. The video examines how EUV and DUV lithography, chip packaging, and logic folding contribute to advancing semiconductor manufacturing capabilities amid hardware competition.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses how to navigate job hunting in tech as AI screening becomes prevalent, emphasizing networking, personal branding, and technical skill-building over mass applications. The video argues that junior developers remain valuable and that jobs will evolve rather than disappear due to AI, drawing parallels to past technological transitions.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers outlines five production deployment patterns for TypeScript AI agents: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, multi-layer authentication with OAuth and IAM isolation, type-safe database access with tenant isolation, guardrails with cost budgets and model routing, and observability via metrics and distributed tracing. The patterns are implementation-agnostic but demonstrated on AWS infrastructure.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: Tutorial on Infrastructure as Code using AWS CDK with Python to build a serverless REST API. Demonstrates IaC principles by contrasting manual AWS console setup with automated deployment using CDK, covering DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: AWS Developers outlines five techniques to prevent AI agent hallucinations and reduce token waste: semantic tool selection to filter context, graph RAG for verifiable structured queries, multi-agent validation to catch failures, symbolic guardrails coded in Python, and runtime steering for self-correction. Demonstrations use a travel agent built with Strands Agents framework and show integration with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore at scale.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech explores whether Google engineers use code generation tools ("vibe code") by comparing OpenAI Codex and Claude Code capabilities, examining current practices at Google in 2024, and speculating about future adoption trends through 2026.

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or project that the presenter argues is fundamentally changing or threatening open source software development. The video explores the mechanisms, ethical implications, and future impact of Malus on open source maintainers.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: "ChatGPT Moment" for Robotics Is Coming. The Real Problem Isn't Intelligence | Stanford, Catie Cuan
videos.summary: Catie Cuan argues that a "ChatGPT moment" is coming for robotics but warns against over-focusing on humanoid robots as the sole vision for the future. She advocates for designing robots prioritizing human interaction and delight over utility alone, suggesting that billions of robots will reshape how we approach robotic design and deployment.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how her AI performance management platform competes against much larger enterprises by building a complete platform rather than incremental features. She reflects on discovering that even Google relied on spreadsheets for strategic planning, and shares hiring and trust-building strategies that enabled Pigment to win customers like Figma, Brex, and Anthropic.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate early customers do not indicate genuine product-market fit, which should instead manifest as pull rather than push, with customers initiating purchasing conversations. He advises founders to embed in customer workflows rather than conduct formal interviews, and to make deliberate bets on future AI capabilities rather than relying on current models alone.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird founded Koah Labs, an ad monetization platform for AI tools, raising $26M by betting on ads rather than subscriptions. He argues that the real bottleneck for AI agents is not technology but human trust adoption, and that ads will be more viable than subscriptions for sustaining AI applications.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
